title: H artifact in 2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv executed: PR #7 opened
content: The H artifact from journal/2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv was executed in a single execution-mode session 2026-05-02. PR #7 (spec/dual-host-deploy → main) was opened on klappy/agent-messaging-service: https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service/pull/7. Diffstat: 9 files, +38/−32. Mergeable=true on creation. Replaced 27 ams.covenant.dev references across SPEC.md (v1.0→v1.1), POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, PROTOCOL.md, AMS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ESSAY.md, README.md, and canon/decisions/D0010-observability-via-subscriber-not-wire.md with the dual-host pattern (ams.klappy.dev + ams.truthkit.ai) per canon/decisions/D0011-multi-host-cname-deployment. POC-INFRA §8.1 wrangler.toml routes block now declares both custom-domain entries. AMS_HOST env var was removed from the [vars] block (see Decision below). README also corrected truthkit.dev → truthkit.ai. SPEC §14 received a dated v1.1 row (2026-05-02) citing both D0011 and D0007. Journal entries from the planning session were preserved as historical evidence — not rewritten — keeping 3 ams.covenant.dev refs in journal/2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv intact. Final clean-grep across all non-journal files shows zero residual ams.covenant.dev references except the SPEC §14 changelog row that names what was replaced (intentional, required for change-log accuracy).

title: Smoke test in SPEC §3.1 stayed single-host; dual-host framing added in section preamble
content: When applying D0011 to the SPEC §3.1 smoke test, the test mechanics were kept single-host (ams.klappy.dev) while dual-host framing was added in the section preamble. Rationale: the H artifact specified replacement of references with the dual-host pattern, not test expansion. Adding a 6th smoke check exercising the second host would have been a scope expansion mid-execution, which is invalid per the operating contract. The framing addition ('the deployed Worker is reachable at both ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai per D0011; the smoke test below exercises the ams.klappy.dev host, and the same Worker is reachable identically via ams.truthkit.ai') is informational not testing. By contrast, POC-INFRA §13 'Done When' did receive a dual-host /healthz check because that section's purpose is to verify the deploy substrate, and per D0011 the substrate is dual-host by definition — checking only one host would silently miss a missing CNAME or route. The §13 change is faithful to the section's stated purpose, not a scope expansion.

title: Removed AMS_HOST env var rather than substituting a hostname
content: D0011 explicitly states 'the wire never reads the host' and 'the Worker reads request.headers.get(host) only when constructing magic links to return; everywhere else, the host is irrelevant.' POC-INFRA §8.1 [vars] block previously declared `AMS_HOST = "ams.covenant.dev"`. A naive hostname substitution to `AMS_HOST = "ams.klappy.dev"` would have re-introduced static per-host config for a substrate that D0011 makes host-blind by design — and would have created ambiguity about which of the two hosts is 'the' AMS_HOST. The cleanest D0007-revision-order alignment was removal: the [vars] block now contains only a comment block citing D0011 explaining why no AMS_HOST var exists. This is a small forward-compatibility win because adding a third or fourth CNAME later requires zero config change. Cross-reference grep confirms AMS_HOST is not referenced anywhere else in the repo, so the removal is safe.

title: Operator's '26 refs across 9 files' was close but observed reality was 27
content: The operator's handoff message stated '26 ams.covenant.dev refs across 9 files'; the planning journal H artifact stated '18 references'. Pre-execution grep observed 27 references across the 9 non-journal files plus 3 in the journal (30 total). All numbers were close but none exact; only direct observation produced the operating count. Reality is sovereign — the executing session counted before editing, used the observed number, applied no fudging. The journal-vs-non-journal distinction was implicit in the operator's '9 files' (one of the 10 grepped files is the journal) and made explicit by reading the H artifact carefully ('replaces all 18 ams.covenant.dev references with the dual-host pattern across [the 9 named files]' — the journal is not in the named files). This is consistent with the principle that historical journals are immutable evidence; rewriting them would falsify the record of why D0011 was needed.

title: Validation of PR #7 by a fresh Claude session is the next executable step
content: Per klappy://canon/principles/verification-requires-fresh-context and the planning session's D artifact 'Validation uses a fresh Claude session with the journal as the handoff surface', validation of PR #7 must happen in a fresh Claude session with cold context. The validating session should: (1) read journal/2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv to understand the H artifact that was the contract; (2) read this execution journal to understand what was actually built; (3) read SPEC §14 and the PR diff (https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service/pull/7) to verify the artifact matches the contract; (4) produce a findings entry with explicit accept | iterate | pivot disposition. Same-session self-review by the executing instance is not validation. After PR #7 merges (human approval expected), POC-PLAN Day 1 (Worker scaffold + accounts + conversations endpoints) becomes the first code-producing step — that becomes the next H artifact for the executing session. Branch was named spec/dual-host-deploy as proposed in the planning journal H artifact.

title: Iteration: PR #6's two improvements cherry-picked onto PR #7
content: After exploration-mode head-to-head comparison of the two open PRs against the H artifact, the operator chose PR #7 (spec/dual-host-deploy) as the merge target with two specific improvements from PR #6 cherry-picked on top: (1) journal/README.md gains an index entry for journal/2026-05-02-spec-dual-host-deploy-execution.tsv (the journal index was lagging — see sibling observation); (2) POC-INFRA.md §11 step 1 gains a 'Repeat against ams.truthkit.ai to verify host parity per D0011' instruction, since the smoke test is the operational verification surface and an empty CNAME or missing route on the second host would otherwise be silently missed at deploy. SPEC §3.1 mechanics intentionally stayed single-host per the prior Decision in this same TSV — the smoke test in the SPEC is the contract, not the operational substrate verification, and that scope discipline holds. PR #6 is being closed in favor of PR #7. No protocol or wire behavior changed.

title: Two sibling 2026-05-02 journal files remain un-indexed in journal/README.md
content: While cherry-picking PR #6's journal/README.md update onto PR #7, observed that 2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv and 2026-05-02-ams-sovee-substrate-principle.tsv (both landed via PR #5, the D0011 merge) are also missing from the journal index. Faithfully mirroring PR #6's update means adding only the line for this PR's execution.tsv, matching what PR #6 added — expanding scope to also index the two sibling entries would be an out-of-scope mid-execution fix. Surfacing here so a follow-up (or the PR #5 author) can close the gap without it being lost. No behavior impact; index hygiene only.
